zotero.abstractNote: We study the adversarial risk of interpolating classifiers in the presence of label noise. We show that for a wide class of models, including linear models and neural networks in the neural tangent kernel regime, the adversarial risk is governed by a simple law: it is the sum of the standard risk and a term that depends on the label noise and the geometry of the data. This law allows us to quantify the trade-off between standard and adversarial risk, and to explain why adversarial training can lead to a decrease in standard accuracy. We validate our theoretical findings with experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets.
zotero.conferenceName: The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)
